what_it_does: This file is a living noticeboard guide that indexes overnight evidence and possibility passes produced on the provider-registry channel, listing ten related board items with brief summaries and pointing a non-technical user (Tim) to a recommended starting set.
observations: Enumerates ten passes, split into deterministic evidence (Passes 2, 3, 5, 6) and fresh sub-agent passes (Passes 1, 4, 7, adjacent-possible).
Each pass is referenced by a stable board://item-... address and a one-line summary of its contents.
Includes a self-correction section: item-5741bd84 refutes a Pass-4 false tension, and item-93afd7b0 corrects the stale-registry cause to a live MCP server rather than agent reads.
States that everything built overnight will go live on the connector at the next MCP reconnect, restricted to Tim.
Notes a verified vision capability: chat-4b is vision-capable and the local image-tagging path uses chat-4b plus downscaling.
Ends with a concrete no-code interaction path for Tim: start with Pass 4 meaning-cards (item-abaaa924) and adjacent-possible (item-c94cdfd3).
standouts: The self-correction identifying the live MCP server as the stale-registry cause, with a Tim-only reconnect implication.
Explicit no-code onboarding instruction for Tim via meaning-cards and adjacent-possible items.
Clear separation between deterministic evidence and speculative/inference-based passes.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
References related artifacts with board://item-... URIs.
Labels the item as type guide and state living.
Structures body as bullet groups by evidence category and includes a 'WHAT TIM CAN INTERACT WITH' section.
No code symbols are present; content is prose and stable identifiers.
concerns: Heavy dependence on externally linked board items means this file loses meaning if those items are inaccessible or change.
Terminology such as MCP server, Tim-only, and meaning-cards is used without local definition.
Does not explain how the ten passes were validated or what 'evidence-only' criteria were applied.
notes: A morning-read index that maps overnight provider-registry passes into stable board links, with a curated entry point for a non-coding decision-maker.
questions: What is the exact validation process that distinguishes deterministic evidence from fresh sub-agent passes?
What do 'meaning-cards' contain and how are they rendered for Tim?
What does 'Tim-only' signify in terms of permissions or roles?
How is the provider-registry channel structured so that these items are queryable and attached?
What triggers the next MCP reconnect and what goes live on the connector?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is an overnight evidence + possibility wake-up index on provider-registry, queryable and evidence-only; the file matches that purpose by listing passes, linking to evidence, and noting corrections.
intention_signals: It appears designed to turn a batch of overnight machine-generated analyses into a scannable, human-readable dashboard that quickly orients a decision-maker and routes them to the most accessible starting points.
novelty: It treats a channel noticeboard as a queryable evidence ledger, explicitly tiers evidence versus possibility, and embeds self-correction relationships directly in the index rather than only in the linked items.
contribution: A compact, link-rich index with evidence-tier labels and a recommended non-code starting path, which a fused system could keep as a human orientation layer over a larger set of board artifacts.
summary_for_embedding: A living noticeboard guide that indexes ten overnight evidence and possibility passes on the provider-registry channel via stable board item links, includes self-corrections, and recommends a no-code starting path for Tim.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a scannable morning-read dashboard that orients a human decision-maker to overnight machine-generated evidence and grounded possibilities.
